source_text: ABF Sugar reported revenue of £2,054m and an adjusted operating loss of £2m in 2025. The report states that Sugar sales declined 10% and that the segment loss was due to low European sugar prices. It further explains that UK and Spanish sales and profitability declined significantly due to persistently low European sugar prices and high beet costs for the 2023/24 campaign.
review_comment: Check whether this should be stored as a financial event or market-risk signal.

investment_currency: GBP million impairment/restructuring costs with Vivergo
capacity_change: reduced footprint from three beet facilities to one
source_text: The report states that Azucarera had a structurally too high cost base and that ABF completed restructuring in northern beet operations to reduce the footprint from three beet facilities to one. It also states that impairment charges and restructuring costs of £161m were recognised in relation to Vivergo and Azucarera.
review_comment: Important for operations, risk resilience and fixed-cost reduction analysis.

source_text: In 2025 ABF made the decision to close the Vivergo bioethanol plant after the UK Government did not provide the regulatory and financial solution required for Vivergo to operate profitably. The report states that Vivergo had sales of £134m and an adjusted operating loss of £36m in 2025, and that closure costs of £30m were recognised.
review_comment: Strong example of regulation-linked profitability risk for bioethanol diversification.

capacity_change: increase cane processing and sugar production
capacity_unit: tonnes additional sugar production annually over five years
source_text: ABF describes a two-phase factory debottlenecking programme at Ubombo Sugar. Operational efficiency improvements are expected to enable 20% more cane to be processed and increase total sugar production by 47,000 tonnes annually over the next five years.
review_comment: Connects directly to investment momentum and operations resilience.

site_name: Bury St Edmunds and Wissington sugar factories
capacity_unit: tonnes CO2e annual reduction potential from named projects
source_text: British Sugar reports decarbonisation projects focused on energy efficiency, steam reduction, renewable resources and fuel switching. The Bury St Edmunds energy reduction project was commissioned in September 2025 and is expected to cut site emissions by 19,500 tonnes CO2e per year. At Wissington, construction is underway on a steam drying project aiming to reduce emissions by 50,000 tonnes CO2e per year from 2026.
review_comment: Representative sustainability-investment event; not an exhaustive list of all ABF Sugar projects.
